description_long: This house is a two-story residential building with a steeply pitched gable roof, surrounded by a dense arrangement of snow-covered evergreen trees. The front facade features a small covered porch with steps leading up to the main entrance, flanked by two large windows and a central arched window above. The exterior walls are light-colored, likely painted wood or siding, and the roof is a uniform gray, possibly shingled. The windows are symmetrically placed on the front, with additional smaller windows visible on the sides and rear. The landscaping is dominated by tall coniferous trees, giving the property a secluded, wintry appearance. The overall design suggests a traditional, perhaps Colonial Revival or Craftsman-inspired style, with simple lines and functional details.
description: A two-story house with a steep gable roof, light exterior, and a covered front porch, surrounded by snow-covered evergreen trees. Symmetrical windows and a central arched window add to its traditional, wintry charm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, main entrance, two large windows, and a central arched window above, framed by tall snow-covered trees.
description_view_1: Angled view from above and to the left, highlighting the roof structure, side windows, and dense tree coverage around the house.
description_view_2: Rear view with minimal window visibility, showing the back wall and the surrounding trees, emphasizing the secluded setting.
description_view_3: Angled view from above and to the right, displaying the side of the house, additional windows, and the thick perimeter of snow-laden evergreens.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with steps, central door, and simple columns, flanked by large windows.
